title: swagger-check: new CI tool to cross-check swagger
body:
New script cross-references r.Handle() and r.HandleFunc()
calls against the preceding '// swagger:operation' comments,
and exits failure (with descriptive error messages) if any
comments do not match the code.

This script should not be necessary: the swagger comments
should be autogenerated from the source code.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Note: This is going to fail. There are still four errors in the swagger code:
```
pkg/api/server/register_containers.go:
-       // swagger:operation POST /libpod/containers/{name}/pause libpod libpodPauseContainer
+       // ................. .... /libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause ......
        r.HandleFunc(VersionedPath("/libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause"), s.APIHandler(compat.PauseContainer)).Methods(http.MethodPost)

pkg/api/server/register_info.go:
-       // swagger:operation GET /info libpod libpodGetInfo
+       // ................. ... ..... compat
        r.Handle(VersionedPath("/info"), s.APIHandler(compat.GetInfo)).Methods(http.MethodGet)

pkg/api/server/register_events.go:
-       // swagger:operation GET /events system getEvents
+       // ................. ... ....... compat
        r.Handle(VersionedPath("/events"), s.APIHandler(compat.GetEvents)).Methods(http.MethodGet)

-       // swagger:operation GET /libpod/events system libpodGetEvents
+       // ................. ... .............. libpod
        r.Handle(VersionedPath("/libpod/events"), s.APIHandler(compat.GetEvents)).Methods(http.MethodGet)
```

@baude, @jwhonce, I cannot resolve those myself. I don't know if I should fix the source code, or add special-case exceptions to my script, or (something else). I need your advice.

@edsantiago @baude A quick look at these shows that they are outliers in as much as there is only one function called for both compat and libpod.  The only swagger v2 solution is to code two swagger stanzas that only differ in that tag.
@edsantiago Have you looking into parsing the yaml as well?  The tags stanza might help here.

>there is only one function called for both compat and libpod

That covers the second one only (`/info`). I can just hardcode an exception there. The next two (3&4, the `/events` ones), should I just hardcode to `system`? And what about the first one (the `{name:..*}')

>Have you looking into parsing the yaml as well?

Is that `pkg/api/tags.yaml`? I don't see much of use in there; what have I missed?

Thank you!

I just (rebased and) pushed a new version with more hardcoded exceptions. The one remaining discrepancy is now:
```
pkg/api/server/register_containers.go:
-       // swagger:operation POST /libpod/containers/{name}/pause libpod libpodPauseContainer
+       // ................. .... /libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause ......
        r.HandleFunc(VersionedPath("/libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause"), s.APIHandler(compat.PauseContainer)).Methods(http.MethodPost)
```
How should I deal with that one? That is: is `:..*` likely to appear again? Should it just be stripped off and converted to plain `{name}`?
Or is that swagger comment actually wrong, and in need of fixing?

Ping @edsantiago, @jwhonce, @baude.

I can't really help on swagger issues.

/hold

Do not merge, more bugs sneaked into master so we have more broken swagger to fix

Force-pushed, now with fixes for broken #5638 

@jwhonce @baude I know you're sick of me complaining but can  we please please please please give some attention to this so we can get it merged? Once again, I am awaiting instructions on what to do about this inconsistency:
```
pkg/api/server/register_containers.go:
-       // swagger:operation POST /libpod/containers/{name}/pause libpod libpodPauseContainer
+       // ................. .... /libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause ......
        r.HandleFunc(VersionedPath("/libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause"), s.APIHandler(compat.PauseContainer)).Methods(http.MethodPost)
```

> Force-pushed, now with fixes for broken #5638
> 
> @jwhonce @baude I know you're sick of me complaining but can we please please please please give some attention to this so we can get it merged? Once again, I am awaiting instructions on what to do about this inconsistency:
> 
> ```
> pkg/api/server/register_containers.go:
> -       // swagger:operation POST /libpod/containers/{name}/pause libpod libpodPauseContainer
> +       // ................. .... /libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause ......
>         r.HandleFunc(VersionedPath("/libpod/containers/{name:..*}/pause"), s.APIHandler(compat.PauseContainer)).Methods(http.MethodPost)
> ```

i see no reason why pause should be different than the others ... remove the :..*
@baude thank you! Fixed, and I now expect tests to go green.

From flake-summarize:

#### test ubuntu-18 ubuntu-18

  2020-04-06T09:21:22  [integration_test](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5164135293911040/logs/integration_test.log)
*   [Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with flag --authfile ](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-login-and-logout-with-flag---authfile--1)
*   [Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --tls-verify ](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-login-and-logout-with---tls-verify--1)
*   [Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with flag --authfile ](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-login-and-logout-with-flag---authfile--2)
*   [[same]](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-login-and-logout-with-flag---authfile--3)
*   [Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --tls-verify ](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-login-and-logout-with---tls-verify--2)
*   [[same]](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-login-and-logout-with---tls-verify--3)
*   [Podman exec [It] podman exec preserve fds sanity check ](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-exec-preserve-fds-sanity-check--1)
*   [[same]](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5164135293911040/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-exec-preserve-fds-sanity-check--2)

The common factor in the login tests is:
```
Error: Error copying image to the remote destination: Error initializing destination docker://localhost:5002/test-apline:latest: error loading registries: error loading registries configuration "test/registries.conf": open test/registries.conf: no such file or directory
```
I don't understand why this passed on re-run; and from a quick glance at the e2e sources I can't figure out how/where/why `test/registries.conf` gets created to see if there's a race somewhere. I sure would appreciate someone looking into this.

The other flake is two instances of:
```
time="2020-04-06T15:10:52Z" level=error msg="failed to close libpod db: \"db file close: close /tmp/podman_test367686297/crio/libpod/bolt_state.db: bad file descriptor\""
         Error: error saving container d0a8298d9c817c05447d2e055b0bce54239b8d924102a9f3659d7ff07154fef1 state: write /tmp/podman_test367686297/crio/libpod/bolt_state.db: bad file descriptor
```
@mheon would you mind taking a look at the boltdb ones?
@edsantiago Can you confirm that the BoltDB one only happens with exec preserve-FDs? I think I have an idea there if so.

@mheon I'm sorry, I can't confirm. This failure is new to me, one I haven't seen before. (I do try to monitor and investigate flakes, and am writing tools to help me do so, but there are still countless flakes that I've never even looked at yet). I will keep my eyes open for it; and if you file an issue (or ask me to) I'll aggregate them there if/as I find more.

To clarify my thoughts there, I think that `podman exec` is closing FDs if `--preserve-fds` is given, and that it's being too aggressive when it does this and also closing the FD BoltDB is using to talk to the database.

/hold cancel

@baude @jwhonce PTAL. I would really like to get this merged so I can get out of the business of manually correcting swagger typos every week.
